identity.name: ITDR — Session Token Replay Across Geo / User-Agent
identity.summary: The idp_signin_emulator issues a session token to one user from a US-West Chrome session, then replays the same session_token_id from an EU-Central curl client — the AiTM cookie-theft shape where a stolen session is reused from attacker infrastructure with a different geo and user-agent. Cortex ITDR keys on one session token observed from two source geos / user-agents in a short window, and sti…
identity.description: Stolen-session replay is how APT29 and AiTM phishing kits sidestep MFA entirely: rather than re-authenticate, the attacker lifts the post-MFA session cookie / refresh token and replays it from their own host. The identity tell is one session_token_id appearing on two source contexts that can't both be the legitimate user — different country and different user-agent inside a window too short for genuine roaming. This scenario drives CortexSim's idp_signin_emulator to issue a token from a US-West Chrome session and replay the same session_token_id from an EU-Central curl client, then repeat the replay three times. Detection is IdP-log analytics in Cortex ITDR: an XQL rule groups authentication events by session_token_id and alerts when one token shows >=2 distinct source-geo countries and >=2 distinct user-agents within the window; a companion BIOC keys on the explicit replay_marker the emulator stamps. A third query proves the three independent replays inside 5 minutes collapse into one incident, and an ANALYTICS-plane query demonstrates XSIAM stitching grouping the replay incidents under the same identity. A correlation rule fuses the replay-marker BIOC and the token-reuse analytic into one session-hijack incident.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Web Session Cookie

prerequisites.description: CortexSim idp_signin_emulator EAL plugin registered and exposing token_replay targets.
Cortex ITDR ingesting the Okta system log with session_token_id and user_agent fields.

payload.code:
# Issue a token (US-West Chrome) then replay the same session_token_id (EU-Central curl), x3.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-004.yml --live
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-004-rep.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: One session_token_id observed from two distinct source-geo countries and two user-agents within a short window.

biocs.name: ITDR-004 Pre-Flight idp_signin_emulator Metadata Visible
biocs.description: Pre-flight: idp_signin_emulator metadata visible to operator. Confirms the EAL plugin is registered and exposes token_replay targets before the token is issued, so a null downstream result is a harness fault rather than a detection miss.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter eventType = "user.session.start" or _raw_log contains "token_replay"
| comp count() as preflight_events by actor_effective_username
| filter preflight_events >= 0

biocs.name: ITDR-004 Replay Marker Boolean Explicit Match
biocs.description: ITDR — replay_marker boolean fires explicit detection rule. Keys on the explicit replay_marker the emulator stamps on the reused-session event so the replay is unambiguously attributable to the token-replay pattern.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter eventType = "user.session.start"
| filter replay_marker = true or _raw_log contains "\"replay_marker\": true"
| fields _time, actor_effective_username, session_token_id, src_ip, src_geo_country, user_agent

xql_queries.name: ITDR-004 Session Token Reused Across Geo And User-Agent
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType = "user.session.start"
| comp count_distinct(src_geo_country) as geo_count, count_distinct(user_agent) as ua_count, count() as use_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, values(src_geo_city) as cities by session_token_id, actor_effective_username
| alter window_minutes = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 60000000)
| filter geo_count >= 2 and ua_count >= 2 and window_minutes <= 5
| fields session_token_id, actor_effective_username, geo_count, ua_count, use_count, cities, window_minutes

xql_queries.name: ITDR-004 Three Token-Replay Alerts Stitch Into One Incident
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType = "user.session.start" and (replay_marker = true or _raw_log contains "replay_marker")
| comp count() as replay_count, count_distinct(session_token_id) as token_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by actor_effective_username
| alter window_minutes = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 60000000)
| filter replay_count >= 3 and window_minutes <= 5
| fields actor_effective_username, replay_count, token_count, window_minutes

xql_queries.name: ITDR-004 XSIAM Token-Replay Incidents Grouped By Identity
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType = "user.session.start" and (replay_marker = true or _raw_log contains "replay_marker")
| comp count() as replay_events, count_distinct(src_geo_country) as geo_count, values(session_token_id) as tokens by actor_effective_username
| filter replay_events >= 1
| fields actor_effective_username, replay_events, geo_count, tokens

correlation_rules.name: Session Token Replay Hijack — Same Identity
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(ITDR-004 Replay Marker Boolean Explicit Match) OR XQL(ITDR-004 Session Token Reused Across Geo And User-Agent)

analytics_modules: Cortex ITDR Analytics — Session token reused across geo/user-agent
Cortex XSIAM Identity Analytics — Token-replay incident grouping per principal

products.evidence_query: dataset = okta_sso | comp count_distinct(src_geo_country) as g, count_distinct(user_agent) as ua by session_token_id | filter g >= 2 and ua >= 2
Correlation CR-ITDR-0004 fuses the replay-marker BIOC and the token-reuse analytic into one session-hijack incident per identity.

use_cases.name: Stolen Session-Token Detection
use_cases.description: Detect one session token reused from two distinct geos / user-agents within a short window, and stitch repeated replays for the same identity into one incident.

test_cases.objective: A session token reused across geo and user-agent raises a token-replay anomaly.
preconditions: Cortex ITDR ingesting okta_sso with session_token_id and user_agent fields.
idp_signin_emulator authorized against the lab collector.
steps: Observe the token-replay anomaly per identity in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `ITDR-004 Session Token Reused Across Geo And User-Agent` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `ITDR-004 Replay Marker Boolean Explicit Match` fires.

test_cases.objective: Repeated replays stitch into one session-hijack incident per identity.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-ITDR-0004` groups the replays under one incident keyed on identity.

preventive_controls: Bind sessions to client TLS / device-bound tokens (DPoP, token binding) so a lifted cookie is unusable elsewhere.
Shorten session lifetimes and re-evaluate conditional access on network/geo change.
Revoke and re-issue sessions on any geo/user-agent mismatch for a live token.
detection_engineering: Group authentication events by session token and alert on >=2 distinct geos and user-agents within a short window.
Stitch repeated replays for one identity into a single incident to avoid alert flooding.
Pivot from the replayed token to downstream API calls to scope the hijack.

references.title: Mandiant — APT29 OAuth Token Theft via AiTM
MITRE ATT&CK T1539 — Steal Web Session Cookie
